mission.title: caddy: Disabling http-to-https redirect from Caddyfile
mission.description: Disabling http-to-https redirect from Caddyfile. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current caddy setup
steps.description:
Verify your caddy version and configuration:
```bash
caddy version
```
This feature requires a working caddy installation.

steps.title: Review caddy configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant caddy configuration:
Is it possible to [disable the HTTP-to-HTTPS redirects](https://caddyserver.com/docs/json/apps/http/servers/automatic_https/disable_redirects/) in Caddyfile?

My Caddyfile is:

```
sourcegraph.test:3443
tls internal
reverse_proxy

steps.title: Apply the fix for Disabling http-to-https redirect from Caddyfile
steps.description:
Hi Quinn! 👋 

It's not possible in the Caddyfile yet -- probably will be in 2.1 -- but for now you can set the HTTP port to something higher that has permission, by putting an options block at the top of your Caddyfile:

```
{
    http_port 8080
}
```
```yaml
sourcegraph.test:3443
tls internal
reverse_proxy localhost:3080
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Disabling http-to-https redirect from Caddyfile" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi Quinn! 👋 

It's not possible in the Caddyfile yet -- probably will be in 2.1 -- but for now you can set the HTTP port to something higher that has permission, by putting an options block at the top of your Caddyfile:

```
{
    http_port 8080
}
```

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
